name: multi-agent-patterns
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "design multi-agent system", "implement supervisor pattern", "create swarm architecture", "coordinate multiple agents", or mentions multi-agent patterns, context isolation, agent handoffs, sub-agents, or parallel agent execution.
capabilities: Explains three core multi-agent patterns: supervisor, peer-to-peer, and hierarchical
Provides Python implementation examples for agent communication and coordination
Teaches context isolation strategies to overcome single-agent limitations
Includes failure mode analysis and mitigation strategies
Offers practical guidance on consensus mechanisms and voting protocols
author: ChakshuGautam
securitySummary: Educational documentation skill for multi-agent AI patterns. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: markdown code fences misidentified as shell execution, table syntax flagged as crypto algorithms, and standard coordination patterns flagged as reconnaissance. No malicious code, credential access, or data exfiltration patterns present.
valueStatement: Single AI agents hit context limits when handling complex tasks. This skill teaches proven patterns to distribute work across multiple agents while maintaining coordination and avoiding common failure modes.
limitations: Educational content only - requires adaptation for production use
Python examples are simplified implementations, not production-ready
Focuses on architecture patterns, not specific framework implementations
Assumes basic understanding of AI agent concepts

useCases.title: Design scalable AI systems
useCases.description: Learn to architect multi-agent systems that handle complex tasks beyond single-agent capabilities

useCases.title: Implement agent coordination
useCases.description: Apply proven patterns like supervisor/orchestrator and peer-to-peer for effective agent collaboration

useCases.title: Compare multi-agent approaches
useCases.description: Understand trade-offs between different coordination patterns and when to use each

promptTemplates.title: Basic Multi-Agent Design
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to design a multi-agent system for [task]. What architecture pattern would work best and why?

promptTemplates.title: Supervisor Pattern Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement a supervisor agent that coordinates specialized workers for [specific task]

promptTemplates.title: Context Isolation Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: How can I isolate context between agents while ensuring they have enough information to complete their subtasks?

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a consensus mechanism for multi-agent decisions that avoids sycophancy and handles disagreements

bestPractices: Design for context isolation as the primary benefit, not organizational metaphor
Implement explicit handoff protocols with clear state passing between agents
Use weighted voting or debate protocols to avoid sycophancy in consensus
antiPatterns: Creating agents based on human roles without considering context isolation needs
Using simple majority voting that treats all agent opinions equally
Allowing unlimited context accumulation in supervisors without checkpointing

faq.question: Which frameworks support these multi-agent patterns?
faq.answer: LangGraph, AutoGen, and CrewAI are popular frameworks. The skill provides framework-agnostic patterns.

faq.question: How much do multi-agent systems increase token usage?
faq.answer: Expect approximately 15x baseline token usage, but this enables handling tasks impossible for single agents.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with existing single-agent systems?
faq.answer: Yes, start by identifying natural decomposition points and gradually introduce specialized agents.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using multiple agents?
faq.answer: The skill teaches local coordination patterns. No external APIs unless you explicitly implement them.

faq.question: What if agents produce conflicting results?
faq.answer: Implement debate protocols and weighted voting. The skill covers consensus mechanisms for disagreements.

faq.question: When should I choose multi-agent over single-agent?
faq.answer: Multi-agent excels at context isolation and parallelization. Single agents are simpler for straightforward tasks.
